msgs.text:
Turn 59 — final dependency audit: the proof is now a compactness theorem, not a case sweep I used only the three files attached in this turn. The problem statement asks whether the quotients ΓS=F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩ and ΓH=F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩ are isomorphic over the fixed quotient G, equivalently whether there is a continuous Φ:ΓS→ΓH with πH∘Φ=πS.

The answer supported by the attached sweep is:ΓS≅GΓH.More strongly, the sweep proves the existence of a continuous automorphism Φ:F⟶F over G such thatΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup, this descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,which is exactly ΓS≅GΓH. The latest attached history explicitly records this stronger statement as the final state.

What Turn 59 adds At this point, another Magnus-layer computation would be the wrong direction. The structural proof has already moved past local obstruction hunting. The proof now rests on a clean dependency chain:Fixed orientation.UseρS:=rS−1.This is the orientation used by the abelianized row calculation, and it is harmless because rS and rS−1 have the same closed normal closure. The history records that finite and profinite substitutions are required to send ρS to rH.

Invertible H1 -start.OverΛ=Z2 [[G]],the source and target linearized rows arewS=(σ+3E,−2),wH=(Eg0+3E−2,σ−E).

The sweep constructs an idempotent-by-idempotent matrix U∈GL2 (Λ) carrying wS to wH; on the dangerous Ef-piece the displayed matrix has determinant s, a unit.All non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled.Once the H1 -matrix is applied, the (1−E)-piece and E(1−f)-piece have unit coefficients, so no higher obstruction can survive there. The only meaningful tower is the residual Ef, mod-2, s=1 Magnus tower.Residual tower closes uniformly.In the residual problem, the obstruction equation isS(Y0,Y1,q)=X2.

The sweep proves the restricted-Lie inductionCm=Lm (m≥5),so every odd layer is an ordinary boundary and every even square quotient is hit by a root transgression W↦W[2]. This closes the residual obstruction tower to all orders.Coefficient tower closes after the normalized (2,t) reduction.On the local Ef-coefficient ring

R≃Z2 [[t]],t=s−1,the raw row(s2+1,s−1)=(2+2t+t2,t)is Nielsen-equivalent to the exact row (2,t). The t-layer is contractible, the first 2-Bockstein secondary maps satisfy Bd↠Hd(2), and higher (2,t)-adic layers are handled by the normalized Koszul–Witt cascade. The history emphasizes that actual pro-2 representatives produce Witt cascades, not a naïve single Frobenius square, and that the triangular Magnus-degree order makes the induction terminate at every finite level.The final certificate block D.3 is closed.The last executable certificate was the A2=R/(4,t), d=4, μ4 -toggle. Turn 54 records the actual stack-rank check rankD5,6,7=60,rank(D5,6,7μ4B4)=61.

Since H4(2)is one-dimensional, this proves that the continuation kernel toggles the fixed μ4=1 scalar to μ4=0.Compactness uses finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary finite quotient automorphisms.For each degree cutoff N and open ideal J⊂Λ, one usesPN,J=P∩(1+IN+JM)and finite solution sets SN,J of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data(y0,y1,cσ,cτ)∈(P/PN,J)4.These data have first layer UmodJ, satisfy the finite cocycle relation for τσ=τ2, and send ρS=rS−1 to rH modulo PN,J. The reduction maps are canonical because the objects are filtered data, not arbitrary automorphisms of quotients.Each finite datum gives an actual finite over-G automorphism.The induced map on P/PN,J has invertible first Magnus layer UmodJ. By the finite Frattini criterion, it is an automorphism of the finite 2-group P/PN,J; hence the full finite extension is mapped automorphically over G. This avoids any Hopficity assumption for the infinite pro-2 operator group P.Inverse-limit compactness gives the global automorphism.The SN,J are finite, nonempty, and functorial under refinement. Compactness gives a compatible family. Passing to the inverse limit produces a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfyingΦ(rS−1)=rH.Therefore Φ identifies the closed normal relator subgroups and descends to ΓS≅GΓH.

Final audit No new structural mathematical gap remains inside the attached sweep. The remaining issue is external reproducibility: the attached history records the verifier outputs and rank certificates, but the actual verifier scripts or full matrices are not included among the current attachments. For a standalone paper, those should be appended explicitly. The latest history says exactly this: the theorem is proved inside the sweep ledger, while a publication-ready appendix should include the verifier code or explicit matrices.So Turn 59 should not reopen any special case. The uniform pattern is already the point: every apparent fixed-gauge obstruction is absorbed by the full lower-plus-continuation torsor, and the global proof is the filtered Nielsen compactness theorem built from the finite nonempty SN,J.

ESTABLISHED: The attached sweep supports a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH. Hence ΓS≅GΓH.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap remains in the attached proof. The only remaining gap is standalone reproducibility: include the verifier scripts or explicit rank matrices, especially the Turn 54 D.3 stack-rank 60→61, in an appendix.

NEXT: Stop searching for new obstruction layers; write the final note as a proof package with the six dependencies above and an appendix containing the finite verifier outputs and matrix/rank certificates.[TURN59-FINAL-DEPENDENCY-AUDIT-ISO]
